shape.note: Fixed circuit shape: M=4, K<=10, each object has N<=8 clustered candidate representative facts, ITER_N=15.

fixed_point.rounding_note: Q16 integers are stored as strings; generator should apply deterministic half-up rounding consistently.

runtime.support_construction: support is constructed inside the circuit from top1_choice
runtime.padding_rule: Only objects o < K are effective; objects o >= K are padded-zero objects.

facts_meta.note: facts[o] are clustered candidate representatives after normalization and per-object clustering.

cluster_members_meta.note: cluster_members are for audit, frontend display, provenance, and cluster explainability only; they do not enter the circuit.

top1_choice_meta.support_source: top1_choice is the basis for constructing support inside the circuit.

params_meta.note: Natural language normalization, candidate clustering, and signed relation construction are performed outside the circuit; the circuit proves TruthFinder aggregation starting from clustered fact indices, top1_choice, imp/conf, dep_avg, and numeric parameters.

support_semantics.rule: For each effective object o and model w, support[o][top1_choice[o,w]][w] = 65536 and all other facts receive 0.
support_semantics.support_patch: removed; support is no longer a trusted external input.

relation_semantics.source: build_cluster_relation_matrix over clustered facts and cluster members
relation_semantics.split_rule: rel > 0 goes to imp_weight; rel < 0 goes to conf_weight = -rel; rel = 0 means no relation.
relation_semantics.imp_conf: imp/conf are generated outside circuit from signed relation and then used inside circuit.

dependency_meta.dep_avg_source: average dependency from each model to other models

truth_selection_meta.note: Multi-truth display is downstream post-processing unless explicitly added to circuit.

provenance.note: normalize / clustering / relation construction are outside circuit and locked by provenance/hash. support is constructed inside circuit from top1_choice.

constraints.top1_index_range: [0, N_o-1] for effective objects; -1 only for padded objects
constraints.imp_conf_exclusive_rule: For each (o,g,f), imp_weight[o][g][f] and conf_weight[o][g][f] must not both be non-zero.
constraints.q16_nonnegative_unbounded_note: Some weights such as trust_prior_strength may exceed 65536.
